args.query:
SELECT "Index", 
       SUM(CASE WHEN "Close" > "Open" THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS up_days,
       SUM(CASE WHEN "Close" < "Open" THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS down_days,
       SUM(CASE WHEN "Close" = "Open" THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS flat_days,
       COUNT(*) AS total_days
FROM index_trade
WHERE "Index" IN ('IXIC','NYA','GSPTSE')
  AND Date BETWEEN '2018-01-01' AND '2018-12-31'
GROUP BY "Index";

exec_args.sql:
SELECT "Index", 
       SUM(CASE WHEN "Close" > "Open" THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS up_days,
       SUM(CASE WHEN "Close" < "Open" THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS down_days,
       SUM(CASE WHEN "Close" = "Open" THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS flat_days,
       COUNT(*) AS total_days
FROM index_trade
WHERE "Index" IN ('IXIC','NYA','GSPTSE')
  AND Date BETWEEN '2018-01-01' AND '2018-12-31'
GROUP BY "Index";

result.preview:
"Indices with more up than down days in 2018 among North American indices:

- IXIC: 44 up days, 31 down days
- GSPTSE: 44 up days, 41 down days

(NYA had 36 up days and 42 down days, so it had more down days.)"

args.answer:
Indices with more up than down days in 2018 among North American indices:

- IXIC: 44 up days, 31 down days
- GSPTSE: 44 up days, 41 down days

(NYA had 36 up days and 42 down days, so it had more down days.)
